report_metadata.cancer_type: Non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) — EGFR exon 20 insertion mutation

treatments.name: Amivantamab + Carboplatin + Pemetrexed (First-Line PAPILLON Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT (PAPILLON, NCT04538664) published in NEJM 2023. Randomized 308 patients. Definitive evidence supporting first-line use. NCCN Category 1 recommendation

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 11.4 months vs 6.7 months (HR 0.40, p<0.001). ORR 73% vs 47%. 18-month PFS 31% vs 3%. Interim OS HR 0.67 (not yet significant). Transforms outcomes in this historically challenging subtype

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved March 2024 for first-line EGFR ex20ins NSCLC. Subcutaneous formulation (Rybrevant Faspro) also approved, reducing infusion time from hours to minutes. Widely available globally

safety_profile.rationale: Infusion-related reactions (66% IV, reduced with SC), rash (86%), paronychia, hypoalbuminemia. Grade 3+ AEs 75%. Venous thromboembolism risk. Manageable but requires monitoring. SC formulation improves tolerability

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically designed for EGFR exon 20 insertion mutations. Patient's exact molecular profile. EGFR-MET bispecific antibody directly targets the driver mutation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Amivantamab is a bispecific antibody targeting both EGFR and MET receptors with immune cell-directing activity. It inhibits ligand binding, promotes receptor endocytosis and degradation, and engages macrophages, monocytes, and natural killer cells through its Fc domain. Combined with carboplatin-pemetrexed chemotherapy for synergistic cytotoxic and targeted effect.
notable_side_effects: infusion-related reactions (66% IV; reduced with SC)
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (March 2024) — first-line standard of care for EGFR ex20ins NSCLC

treatments.name: Amivantamab Monotherapy (Post-Platinum)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1 CHRYSALIS study (NCT02609776, n=81). FDA accelerated approval May 2021 based on single-arm data. Subsequent PAPILLON data supports mechanism. No randomized monotherapy comparator

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 40%, median DOR 11.1 months, median PFS 8.3 months, median OS 22.8 months in post-platinum patients. Meaningful and durable responses in a treatment-refractory population

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved since May 2021. Available as IV infusion and subcutaneous formulation (Rybrevant Faspro). NCCN-recommended subsequent therapy option for EGFR ex20ins

safety_profile.rationale: Rash (86%), infusion-related reactions (66% IV), paronychia, hypoalbuminemia. Grade 3+ AEs 42%. SC formulation significantly reduces infusion reactions. Manageable toxicity profile

biomarker_match.rationale: EGFR-MET bispecific antibody designed for EGFR exon 20 insertions. Perfect match for patient's molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Amivantamab is an EGFR-MET bispecific antibody with immune cell-directing activity. It blocks ligand binding to both EGFR and MET receptors, induces receptor endocytosis and degradation, and activates immune effector cells (macrophages, monocytes, NK cells) through its Fc domain for antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity.
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (May 2021) — subsequent therapy after platinum-based chemotherapy

treatments.name: Sunvozertinib (Zegfrovy) Monotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/2 WU-KONG1B trial (NCT03974022, n=85 primary efficacy). FDA accelerated approval July 2025. Phase 3 WU-KONG28 confirmatory trial ongoing. Breakthrough therapy designation

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 46% (200mg) with median DOR 11.1 months. First-line phase 2 data (WU-KONG15): ORR 73.1%, mPFS 10.1 months, estimated mOS 23.1 months. Oral TKI with meaningful activity in this resistant subtype

accessibility.rationale: FDA accelerated approval July 2025 for post-platinum EGFR ex20ins NSCLC. Oral administration (200mg daily). Available in the US. Phase 3 confirmatory trial ongoing

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3+ TRAEs 35.4%. Diarrhea (8.1% grade 3+), blood CPK increase (10.1% grade 3+), anemia (8.1% grade 3+). ILD/pneumonitis warning. Ocular toxicity reported. Manageable with dose modifications

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically designed for EGFR exon 20 insertion mutations. Oral irreversible TKI with selectivity for ex20ins variants. Perfect biomarker match for this patient

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Sunvozertinib is an oral, irreversible, highly selective EGFR TKI designed to overcome the steric hindrance caused by exon 20 insertion mutations. It covalently binds to Cys797 in the ATP binding pocket, potently inhibiting EGFR exon 20 insertion variants while maintaining selectivity over wild-type EGFR.
treatments.availability: FDA Accelerated Approval (July 2025) — second-line after platinum-based chemotherapy

treatments.name: Carboplatin + Pemetrexed (Platinum Doublet Chemotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 level evidence as control arm in PAPILLON (n=153). Decades of supporting data in NSCLC. NCCN Category 1 for non-squamous NSCLC. Well-established standard

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 6.7 months, ORR 47% in PAPILLON control arm. Historical real-world data: ORR 39%, mPFS 7.1 months. Reasonable but inferior to targeted approaches. 18-month PFS only 3%

accessibility.rationale: Universally available at all oncology centers worldwide. Generic formulations available. Well-established treatment protocols. No biomarker testing required for access

safety_profile.rationale: Standard chemotherapy toxicity: myelosuppression, nausea, fatigue, renal toxicity (carboplatin), cytopenias. Well-characterized and manageable. Pemetrexed maintenance can prolong benefit but adds toxicity burden

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-targeted — active across NSCLC regardless of driver mutation. Works in EGFR ex20ins but not optimized for this molecular subtype. Universal applicability

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Carboplatin is a platinum-based alkylating agent that cross-links DNA, inhibiting DNA replication and transcription. Pemetrexed is a multi-targeted antifolate that inhibits thymidylate synthase, dihydrofolate reductase, and glycinamide ribonucleotide formyltransferase, disrupting folate-dependent metabolic processes essential for cell division.
treatments.availability: FDA Approved — universally available standard of care

treatments.name: Zipalertinib (CLN-081) Monotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2b REZILIENT1 met primary ORR endpoint (n=176). Rolling NDA submission completed February 2026. Phase 3 REZILIENT3 ongoing. Strong phase 2 evidence with regulatory submission

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 35% overall (40% post-chemo only, 24% post-amivantamab). DCR 89%. Median DOR 8.8 months. Median PFS ~12 months in chemo-pretreated cohort. Active even after amivantamab progression

accessibility.rationale: NDA rolling submission completed February 2026. FDA decision pending. Available through clinical trials (REZILIENT1, REZILIENT3). Not yet commercially available

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3+ TRAEs 23%. Most common: rash (80%), paronychia (32%), diarrhea (30%). Only anemia >5% at grade 3+ (10%). Dose reductions 14%, discontinuations 8%. No drug-related deaths. Favorable safety profile

biomarker_match.rationale: Irreversible EGFR TKI specifically designed for exon 20 insertion mutations. Shows activity across near-loop and far-loop insertion subtypes. Exact match for patient's mutation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Zipalertinib is an oral, irreversible, mutant-selective EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor that potently inhibits EGFR exon 20 insertion mutations. It covalently binds to the ATP-binding site of the EGFR kinase domain, overcoming the structural changes caused by exon 20 insertions that confer resistance to standard EGFR TKIs.
treatments.availability: NDA submitted (rolling submission completed February 2026) — available through clinical trials

treatments.name: Furmonertinib Monotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 FAVOUR study (NCT04858958). Received FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation October 2023. First-line ORR 69% is impressive but single-arm data. Phase 3 FURVENT trial ongoing

survival_benefit.rationale: First-line: ORR 69%, mPFS 10.7 months, DCR 96.6%. Previously treated: ORR 40.9-50%, mPFS 5.8-7.0 months. Enhanced BBB penetration may benefit patients with CNS metastases. Strong efficacy signal

accessibility.rationale: FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation but not yet approved. Available through Phase 3 FURVENT trial. Oral administration. Not commercially available in US or EU

safety_profile.rationale: Diarrhea (26.4%), rash (26.4%). No grade 3+ TRAEs reported in available data. Favorable tolerability profile compared to other agents in this class. Well-suited for ECOG 0 patient

biomarker_match.rationale: Third-generation irreversible EGFR TKI with activity against exon 20 insertion mutations. Enhanced BBB penetration. Directly targets patient's driver mutation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Furmonertinib is an oral, irreversible, third-generation EGFR TKI with enhanced blood-brain barrier penetration. It selectively inhibits EGFR kinase activity in tumors harboring exon 20 insertion mutations while maintaining activity against CNS metastases due to its favorable pharmacokinetic properties.
treatments.availability: Investigational — FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation; Phase 3 FURVENT trial recruiting

treatments.name: Datopotamab Deruxtecan (Dato-DXd / Datroway)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 TROPION-Lung01 and Phase 2 TROPION-Lung05 data. FDA accelerated approval June 2025 for pretreated EGFR-mutated NSCLC. Pooled analysis of 117 EGFR-mutant patients

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 45% in pooled EGFR-mutant population. Median DOR 6.5 months. Median PFS 4.4 months vs 3.7 months docetaxel (HR 0.75). Modest but statistically significant improvement in later-line setting

accessibility.rationale: FDA accelerated approval June 2025 for EGFR-mutated NSCLC after prior EGFR-directed therapy and platinum chemotherapy. NCCN-listed as preferred second/third-line option. IV infusion q3w

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3+ TRAEs 25.6% vs 42.1% with docetaxel. Better tolerated than docetaxel. Key risks: stomatitis, nausea, ocular toxicity, ILD. Favorable safety profile for a later-line option

biomarker_match.rationale: TROP2-targeted ADC — not specific to EGFR ex20ins but active across EGFR-mutated NSCLC. Provides a mutation-agnostic later-line option. Data include ex20ins patients in pooled analyses

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Datopotamab deruxtecan is a TROP2-directed antibody-drug conjugate. It consists of a humanized anti-TROP2 antibody linked to a topoisomerase I inhibitor payload (DXd). Upon binding to TROP2-expressing tumor cells, the ADC is internalized and releases DXd, causing DNA damage and cell death. Bystander effect allows killing of neighboring tumor cells.
biomarker_requirements: EGFR mutation (any activating type including exon 20 insertion)
Prior EGFR-directed therapy and platinum-based chemotherapy
treatments.availability: FDA Accelerated Approval (June 2025) — subsequent therapy for pretreated EGFR-mutated NSCLC

treatments.name: JMT101 + Osimertinib (Bispecific Antibody + TKI Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1b/2 BECOME trial data (NCT04448379, n=121 in Phase 1b; NCT05132777, n=112 in Phase 2). Phase 3 trial ongoing (NCT06380348). Early but promising clinical evidence

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 36.4-50%, mPFS 6.9-8.2 months. Intracranial disease control 87.5% in patients with brain metastases. Platinum-refractory: ORR 34%, mPFS 9.2 months. Meaningful activity with CNS penetration

accessibility.rationale: Phase 3 trial ongoing (NCT06380348). Not yet approved in any jurisdiction. Limited to clinical trial access. Osimertinib is individually available but combination is investigational

safety_profile.rationale: Combination of anti-EGFR antibody with TKI. Expected skin toxicity (rash, paronychia) and infusion reactions. Safety profile consistent with individual components. Manageable with standard interventions

biomarker_match.rationale: JMT101 is an anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody providing comprehensive EGFR blockade through signaling inhibition, internalization, and ADCC. Combined with osimertinib for dual mechanism targeting EGFR ex20ins

treatments.mechanism_of_action: JMT101 is an anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody that achieves comprehensive EGFR pathway blockade through three mechanisms: direct signaling inhibition, receptor internalization and degradation, and antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC). Combined with osimertinib (a third-generation EGFR TKI), this dual approach provides orthogonal EGFR targeting.

treatments.name: Amivantamab + Lazertinib (Bispecific + Third-Gen TKI)

evidence_level.rationale: CHRYSALIS-2 Phase 1/2 data for atypical/uncommon EGFR mutations including ex20ins. Approved for EGFR L858R/ex19del (MARIPOSA). Preclinical evidence shows synergy in ex20ins models. Limited ex20ins-specific clinical data

survival_benefit.rationale: Preclinical models demonstrate significant antitumor activity in ex20ins patient-derived xenografts. Clinical data for uncommon EGFR mutations show activity. Lazertinib enhances amivantamab on-target expression. Specific ex20ins survival data limited

accessibility.rationale: Both agents FDA-approved individually. Amivantamab approved for ex20ins. Lazertinib (Lazcluze) approved with amivantamab for L858R/ex19del. Off-label combination for ex20ins feasible at major centers

safety_profile.rationale: Combined EGFR-mediated toxicity from bispecific antibody and TKI. Rash, paronychia, infusion reactions, diarrhea. VTE risk. Higher toxicity burden than monotherapy but manageable. SC amivantamab reduces infusion issues

biomarker_match.rationale: Dual EGFR targeting: amivantamab (EGFR-MET bispecific) plus lazertinib (third-gen EGFR TKI). Lazertinib enhances inhibitory capacity of amivantamab by increasing on-target expression. Rational combination for EGFR ex20ins

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combines amivantamab (EGFR-MET bispecific antibody with immune-directing activity) with lazertinib (a potent third-generation EGFR TKI). Lazertinib provides intracellular kinase inhibition while amivantamab blocks extracellular ligand binding, induces receptor degradation, and activates immune effector cells. Preclinical data show lazertinib enhances amivantamab efficacy by increasing target expression.
biomarker_requirements: May benefit from EGFR and MET co-expression
treatments.availability: Components FDA-approved individually — off-label combination for ex20ins at major cancer centers

treatments.name: Poziotinib (Dose-Optimized)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 ZENITH20 cohort 1 (n=115). Primary endpoint ORR 14.8% not met at 16mg daily. Dose optimization (8mg BID) improved ORR to 31.6% in subsequent cohort. Differential efficacy by insertion location

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 14.8-31.6% depending on dose schedule. Median DOR 7.4 months. Median PFS 5.5-7.2 months. DCR 68.7%. Moderate benefit, inferior to amivantamab or sunvozertinib. Near-loop insertions respond better

accessibility.rationale: FDA Fast Track designation but not approved. Not commercially available. Limited trial access. Development status uncertain after primary endpoint was not met

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity at 16mg daily: diarrhea, rash, stomatitis. High rate of dose reductions. 8mg BID schedule improved tolerability with fewer grade 3+ events. Narrow therapeutic window

biomarker_match.rationale: Pan-HER TKI with activity against EGFR exon 20 insertions. Efficacy depends on insertion location (near-loop vs far-loop). Patient's specific insertion subtype affects expected response

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Poziotinib is an oral, irreversible pan-HER tyrosine kinase inhibitor targeting EGFR, HER2, and HER4. Its small molecular size allows it to fit into the sterically constrained ATP-binding pocket created by exon 20 insertion mutations, inhibiting constitutive EGFR signaling.
biomarker_requirements: Near-loop insertion location may predict better response
treatments.availability: Investigational — FDA Fast Track designation; limited clinical trial access

treatments.name: HER3-DXd (Patritumab Deruxtecan / U3-1402)

evidence_level.rationale: HERTHENA-Lung01 Phase 2 trial (NCT04619004). ORR 29.8%, DCR 73.8% across EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Data include ex20ins patients. Accelerated approval pathway being explored

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 29.8%, median PFS 5.5 months, DCR 73.8%. Provides a later-line option after TKI and chemotherapy failure. Moderate benefit in heavily pretreated population

accessibility.rationale: Investigational. Available through clinical trials. Not yet commercially available. HER3-targeted ADC represents a novel approach for EGFR-mutant NSCLC resistance settings

safety_profile.rationale: ADC class effects: nausea, fatigue, thrombocytopenia, ILD risk (DXd payload). Similar safety profile to other DXd-conjugated ADCs. ILD monitoring required. Manageable in clinical setting

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets HER3 receptor, commonly expressed in EGFR-mutant NSCLC including ex20ins. Not EGFR-specific but leverages HER family co-expression. Provides mutation-agnostic mechanism

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Patritumab deruxtecan (HER3-DXd) is an antibody-drug conjugate targeting HER3 (ErbB3), which is broadly expressed in EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Upon binding HER3, the ADC is internalized and releases a topoisomerase I inhibitor payload (DXd), causing DNA damage. This approach bypasses EGFR mutation-specific resistance mechanisms.
treatments.availability: Investigational — Phase 2 completed (HERTHENA-Lung01); regulatory pathway under evaluation

treatments.name: YK-029A (Next-Generation EGFR Ex20ins TKI)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1 data only with very small sample size (n=26). ORR 73.1% and DCR 92.3% are impressive but require Phase 2 confirmation. Very early clinical development

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 73.1% and DCR 92.3% in Phase 1 are among the highest reported for any ex20ins-targeting TKI. If confirmed in larger trials, could represent a significant advance. No survival data yet

accessibility.rationale: Phase 1 trial only. Very limited access. Years from potential approval. Available only at select investigational sites

safety_profile.rationale: Phase 1 safety data available but limited sample size. EGFR TKI class effects expected. Full safety profile not yet characterized

biomarker_match.rationale: Next-generation TKI specifically designed for EGFR exon 20 insertion mutations. Directly targets patient's driver mutation with potentially improved potency and selectivity

treatments.mechanism_of_action: YK-029A is a next-generation, orally bioavailable EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor engineered with high potency and selectivity against EGFR exon 20 insertion mutations. It is designed to overcome the structural constraints of the mutant kinase domain that limit efficacy of earlier-generation TKIs.
notable_side_effects: EGFR TKI class effects expected (rash, diarrhea, paronychia)

treatments.name: MCLA-129 (EGFR-cMet Bispecific Antibody)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/2 trial (NCT04930432, n=63). Early clinical data with ORR 28.6% and DCR 84.1%. No Phase 3 trial planned yet. Preliminary but encouraging results in pretreated population

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 28.6%, DCR 84.1%, median DOR 7.2 months. Modest ORR but high disease control rate suggests activity. Less robust than amivantamab data. Early-stage evidence

accessibility.rationale: Phase 1/2 clinical trial only. Not approved in any jurisdiction. Limited trial sites. Early development stage

safety_profile.rationale: Infusion reactions (71.9%) are common. Hypoalbuminemia (54.8%). Mostly grade 1-2 AEs. Manageable toxicity profile for a bispecific antibody. No unexpected safety signals

biomarker_match.rationale: Bispecific EGFR-cMet antibody that prevents receptor dimerization. Targets EGFR pathway relevant to ex20ins. Similar mechanism to amivantamab with potentially different binding characteristics

treatments.mechanism_of_action: MCLA-129 is a bispecific antibody that uniquely binds both EGFR and c-Met receptors simultaneously, preventing receptor dimerization and downstream signaling. This dual-receptor blockade inhibits EGFR-driven proliferation while suppressing MET-mediated resistance pathways.

clinical_trials.title: REZILIENT3: Phase 3 Study of First-Line Zipalertinib Plus Chemotherapy in EGFR Exon 20 Insertion-Mutated NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Part A safety lead-in completed; Part B randomization ongoing. Comparing zipalertinib + carboplatin/cisplatin-pemetrexed vs chemotherapy alone. Primary endpoint: PFS

clinical_trials.title: FURVENT: Phase 3 Trial of Furmonertinib in First-Line EGFR Exon 20 Insertion NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 2 FAVOUR data: ORR 69%, mPFS 10.7 months first-line. FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation October 2023. Pivotal phase 3 trial to confirm benefit

clinical_trials.title: Phase 3 Study of JMT101 + Osimertinib vs Cisplatin-Pemetrexed in Advanced NSCLC with EGFR Exon 20 Insertions
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 2 BECOME data: ORR 50%, DCR 79.5%, mPFS 6.9 months. Intracranial control rate 87.5%. Phase 3 comparing JMT101+osimertinib vs chemo

clinical_trials.title: REZILIENT1: Phase 1/2b Study of Zipalertinib in Previously Treated EGFR Exon 20 Insertion NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Primary ORR endpoint met: 35% overall (40% post-chemo only, 24% post-amivantamab). DCR 89%. Median DOR 8.8 months. NDA submitted February 2026

clinical_trials.title: WU-KONG28: Phase 3 Confirmatory Trial of Sunvozertinib vs Chemotherapy in EGFR Exon 20 Insertion NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Confirmatory trial following FDA accelerated approval (July 2025). WU-KONG1B showed ORR 46%, DOR 11.1 months. Phase 3 comparing sunvozertinib 300mg vs chemotherapy. PFS primary endpoint

clinical_trials.title: Phase 1/2 Study of MCLA-129 (EGFR-cMet Bispecific Antibody) in NSCLC with EGFR Mutations
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 28.6%, DCR 84.1%, median DOR 7.2 months in n=63. High disease control rate with manageable safety. Evaluating different EGFR mutation subtypes

clinical_trials.title: Phase 1/2 Study of GB263T (Tri-Specific EGFR-cMET Antibody) in EGFR-Mutated NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 28.6% in n=15 pretreated EGFR ex20ins patients. Tri-specific antibody neutralizes both primary and secondary EGFR mutations and cMET signaling

combination_strategies.rationale: PAPILLON demonstrated that adding amivantamab to standard platinum doublet nearly doubles PFS (11.4 vs 6.7 months, HR 0.40). ORR increases from 47% to 73%. Amivantamab provides EGFR-MET pathway blockade while chemotherapy delivers cytotoxic killing. Current first-line standard of care

Lazertinib enhances amivantamab inhibitory capacity by increasing on-target EGFR expression. Preclinical patient-derived models demonstrate significant antitumor synergy for uncommon EGFR mutations. Dual extracellular and intracellular EGFR blockade. Approved for L858R/ex19del; being explored for ex20ins

Combines oral TKI targeted against ex20ins with standard platinum-pemetrexed backbone. Aims to improve upon chemotherapy-alone outcomes by adding targeted EGFR inhibition. Part A safety lead-in confirmed tolerability. ~312 patients enrolling globally

Comprehensive EGFR blockade via three mechanisms: direct signaling inhibition, receptor internalization/degradation (JMT101), and intracellular kinase inhibition (osimertinib). Intracranial control rate of 87.5% addresses CNS metastasis risk. Phase 3 comparing this combination vs cisplatin-pemetrexed

First-line sunvozertinib monotherapy showed ORR 73.1% and mPFS 10.1 months in WU-KONG15. Combination with chemotherapy may further improve outcomes by providing both targeted EGFR inhibition and cytotoxic DNA damage. Combination trials under development

supportive_care.evidence: Landmark Temel et al. RCT (NEJM 2010, n=151) in metastatic NSCLC. Early palliative care integrated with oncologic care vs standard care alone
supportive_care.benefit: Median OS 11.6 vs 8.9 months (p=0.02) — 2.7-month survival benefit. Better quality of life scores. Fewer depressive symptoms (16% vs 38%, p=0.01). Less aggressive end-of-life care (33% vs 54%)
supportive_care.recommendation_level: Strong — ASCO, NCCN, ESMO all recommend early palliative care integration for metastatic NSCLC

supportive_care.approach: Psychological Support for Anxiety and Depression (CBT, Behavioral Activation)
supportive_care.evidence: ASCO Guideline Update 2023 (JCO). Stepped-care model recommended for cancer survivors with anxiety/depression. Meta-analyses of psychosocial interventions in cancer patients
supportive_care.benefit: Cognitive behavioral therapy and behavioral activation reduce moderate anxiety and depression symptoms. Early supportive care groups show 100% freedom from depression vs standard care. Directly addresses patient's anxiety/depression risk factor
supportive_care.recommendation_level: Strong — ASCO guideline-recommended for moderate symptoms of anxiety/depression in cancer patients

supportive_care.evidence: Multiple RCTs and meta-analyses. Frontiers in Public Health 2022: 8-week exercise program in NSCLC. 2025 pooled analysis of 26 RCTs on exercise effects on depression/anxiety in cancer survivors
supportive_care.benefit: Improves quality of life, functional capacity, and fatigue levels in NSCLC patients. May alleviate depression and anxiety symptoms. Effects vary by patient characteristics and intervention design. Appropriate for ECOG 0 patient who can tolerate moderate-intensity exercise
supportive_care.recommendation_level: Moderate — supported by meta-analyses; benefits for QoL well-established, effects on depression/anxiety more variable

supportive_care.approach: Dermatologic Supportive Care (for EGFR-Targeted Therapy Skin Toxicity)
supportive_care.evidence: Clinical practice guidelines for management of EGFR inhibitor-associated dermatologic toxicities. Pre-emptive skin care protocols studied in multiple trials
supportive_care.benefit: Proactive management of rash (up to 86% with amivantamab), paronychia (32% with zipalertinib), and dry skin prevents dose reductions and treatment discontinuation. Enables maintenance of therapeutic dosing. Critical for maximizing benefit from EGFR-targeted agents
supportive_care.recommendation_level: Strong — essential component of EGFR-targeted therapy management. Pre-emptive moisturizers, sunscreen, topical antibiotics, and dose modification protocols

supportive_care.evidence: MASCC/ESMO antiemetic guidelines. ASCO nutrition support guidelines. Multiple studies on nutritional optimization during lung cancer treatment
supportive_care.benefit: Maintains body weight and nutritional status during chemotherapy and targeted therapy. Reduces nausea impact (common with platinum doublet and ADC therapies). Improves treatment tolerance and quality of life. Weight maintenance correlates with improved OS in NSCLC
supportive_care.recommendation_level: Strong — standard supportive care component for all patients receiving systemic therapy

supportive_care.evidence: PAPILLON trial reported increased VTE risk with amivantamab-chemotherapy. ASCO VTE prophylaxis guidelines for ambulatory cancer patients
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents potentially life-threatening venous thromboembolism, which is an identified risk with amivantamab-containing regimens. Enables safe continuation of first-line therapy without VTE-related interruptions. Khorana score-guided prophylaxis for high-risk patients
supportive_care.recommendation_level: Strong — ASCO guideline-recommended for ambulatory cancer patients on chemotherapy/immunotherapy; particularly important with amivantamab
